headline: Government Surveillance Powers Expanded Without Judicial Oversight
context: Amendments to Official Secrets Act and CMA expanded government electronic surveillance powers without requiring judicial warrants.

cards.text: Surveillance powers expanded without judicial warrant requirements
cards.sub: Amendments allow interception of communications for national security

cards.h: Home Minister can authorize surveillance without court approval
cards.s: Previous framework required AG approval which served as limited check
cards.d: Home Minister can authorize surveillance without court approval Previous framework required AG approval which served as limited check

cards.h: SUHAKAM documented 280 complaints of suspected surveillance in 2025
cards.s: Journalists activists and opposition politicians reported phone monitoring
cards.d: SUHAKAM documented 280 complaints of suspected surveillance in 2025 Journalists activists and opposition politicians reported phone monitoring

cards.h: Bar Council challenged amendments as violating Article 5 liberty protections
cards.s: Constitutional court hearing scheduled for Q3 2026
cards.d: Bar Council challenged amendments as violating Article 5 liberty protections Constitutional court hearing scheduled for Q3 2026

cards.text: Surveillance expansion tests whether security powers can expand without proportional oversight
Surveillance powers expanded without judicial oversight. Executive convenience does not justify bypassing constitutional checks.
